title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

This choropleth map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for 2019, utilizing a color scale ranging from dark red for negative values to dark blue for positive values. The legend indicates a spectrum from -4 to 4, where large positive scores signify that abuses are rare and large negative scores indicate they are widespread. In the map, Canada, Greenland, and several nations in Northern Europe, including Norway and Sweden, appear in shades of blue, corresponding to positive scores between 1 and 4. Conversely, a band of countries across North Africa and the Middle East, including Algeria, Libya, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Yemen, are shaded in darker orange and red tones, representing negative scores generally between -1 and -3. Myanmar is also depicted in a dark red shade, indicating a score near the lower end of the scale. Much of South America, Southern Africa, and parts of Asia are colored in light orange or pale blue, clustering around the neutral 0 mark or slightly into the positive and negative ranges. The United States and Mexico are shown in light orange, falling within the 0 to -1 range. Regions such as central Africa and parts of the Middle East are left white or hatched, denoting areas where no data is available. The scores presented are based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources.
